summary: Current main at aa498cfe11c162ec2621f4b61935835b4312b1ab still has the lossy legacy openai-codex plugin-entry migration. #93897 remains the canonical contributor PR, but it is not merge-ready because review comments identify inaccurate change reporting, silent conflict handling, and missing regression/real-behavior proof; the PR also has failing Real behavior proof and checks-node-core-fast. Planned path is to repair #93897's editable contributor branch and preserve @lsr911 credit.

actions.reason: Repair the editable contributor branch before any merge reconsideration.
Create an executable repair plan for #93897 rather than a merge or close action, both of which are blocked by job frontmatter.
Already-closed refs must not receive closure actions.
Separate plugin-loader boundary work, not a duplicate or replacement for #93897.
Documentation PR for a gateway runtime boundary decision is outside this repair cluster.
Separate diagnostics config feature, not part of this narrow doctor migration repair.
Already closed and unrelated to the #93897 doctor repair.
Related migration family, different root cause and surface.
Related Codex/OpenAI migration area, different fix path.
Related migration/auth family, not duplicate of the plugin-entry migration defect.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair #93897 on the contributor branch so legacy plugins.entries.openai-codex data is merged into plugins.entries.openai without silent loss, reports an accurate merge/rewrite message, and warns when canonical and legacy scalar values conflict.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(doctor): preserve legacy openai-codex plugin entry fields
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
- Repair #93897 so dual `plugins.entries.openai` and `plugins.entries.openai-codex` configs merge without silently dropping legacy-only fields.
- Report accurate change messages for legacy-only rewrite vs dual-key merge paths.
- Surface true scalar key conflicts as warnings/change messages so canonical-wins behavior is visible.
- Preserve contributor credit for @lsr911's source PR #93897 and @Nas01010101's issue/review context in #93891.

## Repair Notes
This should update the existing #93897 branch, not replace it, because `maintainer_can_modify=true` and the diff is narrow. Current main still has the lossy boolean `rewritePluginEntries` path in `src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.runtime.providers.ts`.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- Re-run the real-behavior-proof workflow for #93897 after the repair is pushed.

Refs: #93897, #93891
credit_notes: Repair source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897 by @lsr911; keep that branch as the main lane so commit/PR history preserves contributor credit.
Issue and review context: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93891 by @Nas01010101; review comments on #93897 require message accuracy, conflict warnings, tests, and real behavior proof.
No CHANGELOG.md edit for a normal PR; include release-note context in the PR body/squash message because the doctor migration behavior is user-visible.
